meta_title: Roofing in Inverrary, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Inverrary, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates—call today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacements & Repairs for Inverrary Homeowners
intro:
Inverrary is one of Broward County's most established residential communities, anchored by the iconic Inverrary Country Club and surrounded by well-kept neighborhoods like Inverrary Village that have stood for decades. With average home values around $280,000 and roughly half of all homes built before 1994, many Inverrary properties are carrying roofs that are well past their prime—roofs that were installed before modern hurricane-rated standards became the law of the land in South Florida. That combination of aging infrastructure and a high-wind environment makes professional roofing services not just a smart investment, but a necessary one for protecting your home and everything inside it.

Located in the heart of Broward County with ZIP code 33319, Inverrary sits approximately seven miles from the coast—far enough to avoid direct tidal concerns, but close enough to feel the full force of any major hurricane that rolls through Southeast Florida. The area is designated as a 170 mph wind zone, meaning every roofing system installed here must be engineered and rated to withstand serious storm conditions. Even though Inverrary falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that covers Miami-Dade and coastal Broward, the Florida Building Code still requires hurricane-rated roofing products for all installations and replacements in this area. That means cut-rate materials simply won't pass inspection—and they certainly won't protect your family when the next storm arrives.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped more than 500 homeowners across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties upgrade to roofing systems built for South Florida's demanding climate. We understand the unique challenges Inverrary residents face: older homes with outdated sheathing, mature tree canopies that can create debris hazards, and the kind of hot, humid weather that accelerates wear on lesser roofing materials year after year. Whether you own a golf-view property near the Inverrary Country Club or a family home tucked into Inverrary Village, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the expertise, code compliance, and quality craftsmanship your home deserves.

section_why.h2: Why Inverrary Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Projects
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built a reputation across Broward County as a company that delivers on its promises—every project, every time. That reputation doesn't happen by accident. It's the product of over 60 years of combined installation experience among our team members, a strict 100% in-house installation policy, and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau that reflects our unwavering commitment to quality work and honest communication. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not handing your roof off to a subcontracted crew who may have never worked in your neighborhood. You're getting our own trained, licensed, and insured professionals on your property from day one to project completion.

Our 100% in-house approach matters especially in a community like Inverrary, where many homes were built in the 1970s and 1980s and may present unique structural considerations during a roof replacement. Older homes sometimes have deteriorated decking, undersized rafters, or outdated underlayment systems that need to be addressed before a new roof goes on. Our experienced crews know what to look for and how to handle those situations correctly—and because we manage every aspect of the project ourselves, there are no handoff errors, no miscommunications between contractors, and no shortcuts taken when no one is looking.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also handles the permitting process from start to finish through the Broward County Building Division, so you never have to navigate the paperwork maze on your own. We're intimately familiar with Broward's inspection requirements and code standards, which means your project moves through the approval process efficiently and without costly delays. Our clients in Inverrary, Lauderhill, Tamarac, and Sunrise trust us because we treat every home as if it were our own—and we back that commitment with financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making a quality roof replacement accessible for middle-income households throughout the area.

section_history.h2: Inverrary's Storm History Makes a Strong Case for Hurricane-Rated Roofing
section_history.body:
Inverrary has endured nine named storms since 1990, and three of those left lasting marks on the community and the broader South Florida region. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for roofing standards across Florida—its 165 mph winds devastated thousands of homes and exposed just how inadequate pre-code roofing systems truly were. While Andrew's center tracked south of Broward County, the storm's outer bands still produced damaging winds and debris that tested every roof in the region. Many homes in Inverrary that survived Andrew with minor damage were already operating on borrowed time.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 struck Broward County more directly and with prolonged force, producing sustained hurricane-force winds and widespread roof damage throughout communities like Inverrary, Tamarac, and Sunrise. Wilma exposed a pattern that insurance adjusters saw repeatedly: homes with roofs installed before Florida Building Code updates suffered far greater damage than those with modern hurricane-rated systems. Shingles were torn away, underlayment was compromised, and water intrusion caused interior damage that cost homeowners tens of thousands of dollars beyond the roof itself. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced that lesson, delivering tropical storm and near-hurricane conditions across Broward County and stressing roofs that had already aged another decade since Wilma.

For Inverrary homeowners—particularly those in older homes built before 1994—these storms represent more than historical footnotes. They are a clear reminder that a strong, properly installed, hurricane-rated roof is the single most important line of defense your home has against South Florida's unpredictable and sometimes violent weather seasons. Waiting until the next storm is never the right strategy.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements Every Inverrary Homeowner Needs to Know
section_code.body:
Inverrary falls under the jurisdiction of the Broward County Building Division, which enforces the Florida Building Code—one of the most stringent residential construction standards in the United States. While Inverrary is not designated as a High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), the code still mandates that all roofing products installed in this wind zone meet hurricane-rated performance standards. That means roofing systems must be tested and approved to withstand the 170 mph design wind speeds applicable to this area of Broward County. Any roofing contractor who tells you otherwise—or who attempts to install unapproved materials to save costs—is putting your home, your insurance coverage, and your safety at risk.

For full roof replacements in Inverrary, the Florida Building Code requires that permits be pulled through the Broward County Building Division before work begins. This isn't a formality—it's a critical quality control step that ensures your new roof is inspected by a licensed county official before it's considered complete. The code also specifies requirements for underlayment systems, nail patterns, fastener types, and deck attachment methods. In many older Inverrary homes, the existing roof decking may need to be upgraded to meet current code requirements before a new roofing system can be installed—something a qualified roofing company will identify and address as part of the replacement process.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every permit application, scheduling, and inspection coordination with the Broward County Building Division on your behalf. Our crews are fully trained on current Florida Building Code requirements, and we never cut corners on compliance. Every project we complete in Inverrary results in a properly closed permit—documentation that protects your home's resale value and confirms to your insurance carrier that your roof meets current code standards.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake: Premium Roofing Systems Built for Inverrary's Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading roofing brands that are perfectly suited to the demands of Inverrary's wind zone and weather patterns: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. These aren't off-the-shelf materials—they are rigorously tested, Florida Product Approved, and engineered to perform in South Florida's unique combination of high humidity, intense UV exposure, heavy rainfall, and hurricane-force winds.

The CertainTeed Landmark Series is one of the most respected architectural shingle lines in the country, and it's a standout choice for Inverrary homeowners looking for durable, beautiful, and cost-effective roof protection. Landmark shingles feature a dual-layered construction that provides exceptional wind resistance, holding up to the rigorous performance standards required by the Florida Building Code. They're available in a wide range of colors and profiles, making it easy to complement the architectural style of homes throughout Inverrary Village and the Inverrary Country Club area. CertainTeed's SureStart Plus limited warranty also provides long-term peace of mind, covering both materials and workmanship under qualifying installation conditions.

For homeowners seeking the timeless aesthetic and outstanding longevity of tile roofing, West Lake concrete and clay tile systems are an excellent investment. Tile roofing has long been associated with South Florida's architectural identity, and West Lake delivers products that meet that tradition while exceeding modern hurricane-resistance requirements. Concrete tile is particularly well-suited to Inverrary's inland environment, offering superior resistance to wind uplift, impact from debris, and the relentless heat that characterizes Broward County summers. With a lifespan that can exceed 40 to 50 years when properly installed, West Lake tile roofing is one of the highest-return investments an Inverrary homeowner can make.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Inverrary—and How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
For Inverrary homeowners with properties averaging around $280,000 in value, a full roof replacement is one of the most impactful investments you can make in your home's long-term worth and structural integrity. The cost of a new roof in this area varies based on the size of your home, the pitch of your roof, the materials selected, and any necessary decking repairs discovered during installation. Generally speaking, homeowners in Inverrary can expect architectural shingle replacements to represent a significant but manageable investment, while tile roof systems—given their premium materials and longer installation process—typically carry a higher upfront cost that is offset by decades of additional service life.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes quality roofing accessible to middle-income households throughout Inverrary with flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down. Our financing programs are designed to help homeowners move forward with a necessary project without depleting savings or waiting for the ideal financial moment—which in South Florida's storm-prone environment, may never come at a convenient time. A new hurricane-rated roof is also one of the few home improvements that can pay for itself relatively quickly through insurance premium reductions and avoided storm damage repair costs.

From a return-on-investment perspective, a new code-compliant roof in Broward County typically recovers a strong percentage of its cost in resale value—especially when buyers and their inspectors see a permitted, properly installed system with documentation. Combine that with the insurance savings described in the next section, and many Inverrary homeowners find that the true net cost of a new roof is considerably lower than the sticker price suggests. Our team will walk you through a full cost breakdown and financing options during your free estimate appointment.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Costs in Inverrary
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for replacing an aging roof in Inverrary is the impact it can have on your homeowners insurance premiums. Florida insurance carriers—many of whom have tightened their underwriting standards dramatically in recent years—place enormous weight on the age, condition, and rating of your roof when calculating your premium. Homes with older roofs, particularly those approaching or exceeding 20 years, often face dramatically higher rates or even policy non-renewals. A new hurricane-rated roof installed to current Florida Building Code standards can fundamentally change how insurers view your property.

In Broward County, the wind mitigation inspection is the key tool homeowners use to document their roof's hurricane-resistant features and qualify for insurance credits. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector evaluates your new roof and certifies elements such as the roof covering type, the deck attachment method, the roof-to-wall connection, and the opening protection on your home, that report is submitted to your insurance carrier to unlock meaningful discounts. Insurance credits from wind mitigation reports in Broward County can reduce wind coverage premiums by 20% to 45% or more depending on how many qualifying features your home has—and a new code-compliant roof is the foundation of nearly every strong wind mitigation report.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates the post-installation wind mitigation inspection process for our Inverrary clients, and we install every roofing system with an eye toward maximizing the wind mitigation credits available to you. Our team is familiar with the documentation and construction methods that matter most to Florida's major carriers, and we'll ensure your new roof gives you every possible advantage when it comes time to renegotiate your insurance policy. For many Inverrary homeowners, the annual insurance savings alone make the financing payment on a new roof feel minimal by comparison.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Inverrary home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: The best way to determine whether your Inverrary home needs a full replacement or targeted repairs is with a professional inspection. Key indicators of a roof nearing end-of-life include missing or curling shingles, granule loss in gutters, visible daylight in the attic, persistent leaks after repairs, and a roof age of 20 years or more. Given that roughly 50% of homes in Inverrary were built before 1994, many properties in the area are at or past this threshold. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections to assess your specific roof condition and recommend the most cost-effective path forward.

faqs.q: What does roofing financing look like for homeowners in Inverrary?
faqs.a: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down for qualified homeowners. Our financing options are designed to make a code-compliant, hurricane-rated roof replacement accessible without requiring you to pay the full cost upfront. We work with lenders who understand the South Florida home improvement market and offer competitive rates with straightforward approval processes. During your free estimate, our team will review financing options that fit your budget and project scope so you can move forward with confidence.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Inverrary, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all full roof replacements in Inverrary, and the permit is issued through the Broward County Building Division. The permit process ensures your new roof is inspected for code compliance before the project is considered officially complete. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application, scheduling, and inspection coordination on your behalf. You won't need to navigate the county's permitting system yourself—we manage it from start to finish, ensuring your project closes with a proper permit on record.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement typically take for a home in Inverrary?
faqs.a: Most single-family roof replacements in Inverrary are completed within one to three days for shingle systems and three to five days for tile roofing, depending on the size and complexity of the roof. Factors like decking repairs, permit scheduling, and weather can affect the timeline. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's 100% in-house crews work efficiently and communicate every step of the process so you're never left wondering about the status of your project. We coordinate inspection scheduling with Broward County promptly to avoid unnecessary delays in finalizing your permit.

faqs.q: Will a new roof really lower my homeowners insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes—significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof that meets Florida Building Code standards qualifies your home for a wind mitigation inspection, and the resulting report can unlock insurance discounts of 20% to 45% or more on your wind coverage premium. Florida insurers heavily weight roof age, materials, and installation method when pricing policies, and many carriers have become increasingly unwilling to insure older roofs at any price. Replacing your roof with a code-compliant system from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing not only protects your home—it protects your ability to maintain affordable insurance coverage in Broward County.
